mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward 60-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive during industrial production declines, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when the index falls below 95.0.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index readings above 330.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1.5% within 5 trading days following CPIAUCSL breaches of 330.0, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate consumption, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months with payroll growth, or the correlation between payroll changes and equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and falling retail sales signals a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a prolonged contraction signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, triggering short covering and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Weekly returns remain flat or negative during industrial production rebounds, or the signal fails to generate alpha when the index rises less than 0.5% month-over-month.
